meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve Slow Dev Speed
content.seo_description: Learn how to master project management and overcome slow development speed. An advanced-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for student builders.
content.answer_block: To eliminate slow development, treat your codebase like a product, not a script. Implement agile sprint cycles using Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for automated implementation. By breaking features into atomic, verifiable units, you reduce context switching and accelerate your deployment velocity from weeks to hours.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a terminal, cursor blinking in the void, feeling the weight of a feature list that seems to grow faster than your code. The 'Student Trap' is real: you spend more time wrestling with boilerplate and architectural indecision than actually building the transformative AI features you envisioned. Your development speed isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of a structured engineering system.

Today, we stop the aimless grinding. We are replacing chaos with a high-velocity engineering pipeline. By integrating Antigravity’s architectural foresight with the raw execution power of Claude Code, you will transform your workflow from a manual slog into a streamlined, automated factory. It’s time to build like a senior engineer.

steps.title: Architectural Blueprinting
steps.description: Before writing code, define your system boundaries and data schemas to prevent scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project requirements for a [Project Name] and generate a modular folder structure, a comprehensive data schema, and a list of necessary API endpoints. Focus on scalability and clean architecture.

steps.title: Atomic Task Decomposition
steps.description: Break your project into granular tasks that can be completed in under 90 minutes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Take my project roadmap and break it down into atomic tasks. For each task, provide a clear acceptance criteria and the technical stack required to complete it.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a list of 5 atomic tasks for the authentication module based on the current architecture.'

steps.title: Automated Boilerplate Generation
steps.description: Use AI to scaffold your code to avoid repetitive setup tasks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the boilerplate for the authentication service using Express.js and TypeScript, following the clean architecture pattern we defined earlier.

steps.title: Iterative Implementation Cycles
steps.description: Execute one task at a time using Claude Code's iterative feedback loop.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current implementation of the user controller. Identify potential edge cases and suggest improvements for error handling.

steps.title: Automated Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure new features don't break old ones by automating your test suite.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for the newly created services using Jest. Ensure all edge cases defined in step 2 are covered.

pro_tips: Always define your 'Definition of Done' for every task to avoid the perfectionist's trap of endless refactoring.
Use Claude Code's 'memory' feature to maintain context across multiple sessions, saving you from re-explaining your architecture.
Prioritize building the 'happy path' first, then use Antigravity to identify and patch security vulnerabilities and edge cases.
Treat your CLI commands as documentation; keep a 'commands.md' file in your root to track your progress and automate recurring tasks.
Deploy early and often—even to a staging environment—to validate your project management strategy against real-world deployment hurdles.
content.next_steps: After finishing your first module, perform a 'Velocity Audit' to see how many hours you saved, then refactor your workflow based on where you felt the most friction.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to outpace your peers and build professional-grade software. The only thing missing is consistent execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
